meta.title: Jonathan Brownstein — Manager, Sales Reporting & Analytics
meta.description: Senior insurance and financial-services analytics leader with 15+ years of regulated-industry experience and seven years inside the Data Analytics Office at Hannover Reinsurance Company, where I run the predictive models

hero.description: Senior insurance and financial-services analytics leader with 15+ years of regulated-industry experience and seven years inside the Data Analytics Office at Hannover Reinsurance Company, where I run the predictive models, KPI dashboards, and executive reporting that actuarial, valuation, finance, and risk leaders use to make decisions. Comfortable owning multiple concurrent reporting and analytics initiatives end to end, leading analysts and engineers on delivery, and managing outside vendors and consultants. Quantitative degree path (MS Economics, Applied Econometrics certificate), FLMI from LOMA, and a hands-on toolkit across Python, SQL, advanced Excel and VBA, Databricks, Power BI, Tableau, and SSRS. Track record includes the classification machine-learning rebuild of a regulated reporting workflow recognized by the company's former CEO and the automated ARIMA forecasting platform for Net Amount at Risk that surfaces anomalies before period close.

highlights.label: KPI dashboard design and delivery (Power BI, Tableau, SSRS)
Sales trend, variance, and anomaly analysis

items.title: Senior Data Analyst III
items.context: Technical lead and senior analytics owner inside the Data Analytics Office
items.intro: Senior individual contributor and technical lead inside the Data Analytics Office of a global life and health reinsurer. Lead the predictive modeling, KPI dashboards, and executive reporting the actuarial, valuation, finance, and risk teams rely on, lead analysts and engineers on multiple concurrent initiatives, and own the outside vendors and consultants who support delivery.

subsections.title: Reporting, Dashboards, and Executive Narrative
bullets: Build and maintain the Power BI, Tableau, and SSRS dashboards actuarial leadership, finance, and the C-suite use to read KPIs and movements. Translating model output and complex data movements into a clean executive narrative is the part of the role leadership leans on most.

Designed the firm's first valuation-extract comparison architecture, a repeatable framework for period-over-period movement analysis across time periods and source systems that other teams have since extended.

Set engineering and analytics standards on metadata, lineage, data quality, and audit controls across the Data Analytics Office, and architected the Field Inventory Report that became the data-lineage standard for the Strategic Data Warehouse.

subsections.title: Predictive Modeling and Anomaly Detection
bullets: Led the classification machine learning rebuild of the Same-As-Link (SAL) regulated reporting workflow, the firm's record-to-standard-identifier mapping problem. Accuracy, consistency, and audit readiness all improved measurably, and the company's former CEO recognized the result.

Designed and deployed an automated ARIMA forecasting platform for Net Amount at Risk (NAR) on a scheduled production pipeline. Surfaces anomalies and trend movement before period close so actuarial and finance leaders can react in time.

Built Probability Density Function outlier-detection frameworks across platforms processing more than a trillion records, catching attribute drift in production data before it reaches downstream reports and decision-makers.

subsections.title: Leadership, Mentorship, and Cross-Functional Delivery
bullets: Lead teams of analysts and engineers on the initiatives above, running architecture and design review, code review, and delivery checkpoints across concurrent projects.
Mentor and develop analysts and engineers on Databricks, Spark, SQL, and Python analytics. Comfortable stepping into formal people management on the right team.

Select and manage the outside vendors and consultants who support analytics and reporting delivery. Own scope, timelines, and quality end to end, and partner with governance and compliance to keep the work defensible.
Partner across actuarial, valuation, finance, risk, IT, BI, and compliance to turn business questions into governed analytics and reporting deliverables on a deadline.

items.title: Assistant Business Controller
bullets: Led cross-functional analytics and reporting modernization with HR, Sales, Marketing, Finance, and executive leadership, feeding operational and financial strategy at the regional leadership level.

Designed automated KPI dashboards and reporting frameworks that improved data accessibility and consistency across sales and operations, and established the data-governance and QA practices the team built on.
Picked and managed outside vendors and consultants on analytics, reporting, and finance-systems projects, with ownership of scope, timelines, and delivery quality.

groups.title: Reporting and Dashboards: Power BI, Tableau, SSRS, advanced Excel and VBA, executive-level written and oral briefing
items: Analytics and Modeling: classification machine learning, ARIMA and time-series forecasting, statistical outlier and anomaly detection, data mining, applied econometrics, Python ML stack (pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn, statsmodels), Databricks ML
Languages and Platforms: Python, SQL, R, Databricks lakehouse, Delta Lake, Spark, enterprise data warehousing, cloud data platforms (Azure-leaning), ETL and ELT pipelines
